- avoid a duplicate provider load in-flight probe for direct provider hook-list resolution

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `pnpm openclaw agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof pnpm openclaw agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the CLI run and request-log assertion:

```text
[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] startup stages: runId=c43c486b-23d0-4e83-8fd1-c0dd12f67cba sessionId=proof-subagent-session phase=attempt-dispatch totalMs=5818 stages=workspace:1ms@1ms,runtime-plugins:5605ms@5606ms,hooks:6ms@5612ms,model-resolution:113ms@5725ms,auth:67ms@5792ms,context-engine:1ms@5793ms,attempt-workspace:1ms@5794ms,attempt-prompt:0ms@5794ms,attempt-runtime-plan:24ms@5818ms,attempt-dispatch:0ms@5818ms
```

```json
{
  "head": "25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": ["exec", "read"],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix(gateway): emit subagent_ended hook for api.runtime.subagent.run() (#59164)
target.body:
## Summary

- Plugin SDK subagent runs dispatched via `api.runtime.subagent.run()` never reached `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`, so plugins listening on `api.on("subagent_ended", ...)` were silent for every plugin-spawned run. `sessions_spawn` and ACP subagent flows already call `registerSubagentRun` themselves after spawning; the plugin SDK seam now mirrors that pattern after the gateway dispatch in `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run`.
- Registration is best-effort (a registry failure logs and continues so the public SDK call still returns `runId`), is dynamically imported to keep `server-plugins.ts` off the static back-edge of `subagent-announce.runtime.ts`, and uses the existing gateway request scope to derive a requester key from the configured main-session alias (the same default `resolveSubagentSpawnOwnership` uses when no caller agent session is provided).
- Public `api.runtime.subagent.run()` signature is unchanged. No new SDK exports, no protocol-version bump, no config additions.

## Linked context

Closes #59164

## ClawSweeper P1 follow-up (2026-05-29)

### Session retention fix

ClawSweeper flagged a regression in the original commit: the helper registered runs with `cleanup: "delete"`, which made the registry auto-delete the child session as soon as `subagent_ended` fired. The documented plugin flow is `api.runtime.subagent.run()` → `waitForRun()` → `getSessionMessages()`; auto-delete would race the transcript read and break that contract for existing plugins. The follow-up commit switches the default to `cleanup: "keep"` so the child session survives completion. Explicit teardown stays with the plugin via `api.runtime.subagent.deleteSession()` — same readability default as `sessions_spawn`. The hook still fires in `completeSubagentRun` because `expectsCompletionMessage: false` keeps it on the immediate-emit branch; `cleanup` only governs whether the session is deleted afterward (`src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts:1113-1131`, `src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts:901-929`).

### Regression test

`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` now includes a new test `"preserves the child session so the transcript stays readable until the plugin deletes it"` that exercises the full contract end-to-end against a mocked gateway router: `run()` → `waitForRun()` → `getSessionMessages()` (asserts transcript still readable) → `deleteSession()` → `getSessionMessages()` (asserts the session is now gone). The existing `cleanup` assertion was updated from `"delete"` to `"keep"`.

### Real plugin harness proof

Standing up a live plugin harness against a running gateway is out of scope for this contributor — I do not have a Crabbox/Testbox lane plus a representative plugin install to drive `api.runtime.subagent.run()` against the patched runtime end-to-end. The new regression test is the strongest signal that the plugin contract (run → wait → read → delete) is preserved on the patched adapter, combined with the existing `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts` and `subagent-registry-completion.test.ts` coverage of the registry side.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `subagent_ended` hooks did not fire for runs dispatched through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`. Root cause confirmed at source: `src/gateway/server-plugins.ts` plugin SDK `run` adapter dispatched to gateway `agent` (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` `dispatchAgentRunFromGateway` → `agentCommandFromIngress`) but never created a `SubagentRunRecord`, so `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` (`src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.ts`) was never called when the run finished.
- Real environment tested: vitest unit tests inside this worktree, against the patched runtime adapter; no real plugin harness against a running gateway. The clawsweeper:source-repro evidence on #59164 traces the same call graph the test exercises.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-read-context.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.resume.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`
  - `pnpm format:check src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit` (only pre-existing, unrelated error in `src/secrets/path-utils.ts`)
- Evidence after fix:
  - new test file `src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` — 5 tests pass (includes the new run -> waitForRun -> getSessionMessages -> deleteSession regression)
  - existing `server-plugins.test.ts` + `server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts` — 2 files, 46 tests pass
  - subagent-registry sibling-surface tests — 19 files, 254 tests pass
- Observed result after fix: `api.runtime.subagent.run()` returns the same `{ runId }` as before, AND the registry now holds a `SubagentRunRecord` for the run with `cleanup: "keep"`. When the agent completes, the lifecycle controller calls `emitSubagentEndedHookForRun` → `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` and the `subagent_ended` hook fires; the child session stays alive so the plugin can read it back via `getSessionMessages()` and tear it down with `deleteSession()` when ready.
- What was not tested: did not stand up a real plugin harness against a running gateway end-to-end (see "Real plugin harness proof" above). The reporter's source-repro on #59164 plus the registry's existing completion path cover the rest of the journey.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: vitest-only in this worktree; no Crabbox/Testbox run attached.
- Before evidence: clawsweeper:source-repro on #59164 already shows the pre-patch call graph (no `registerSubagentRun` on the plugin SDK path).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-read-context.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.resume.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
pnpm format:check src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` covers:
- `runtime.run()` calls `registerSubagentRun` with the gateway-returned `runId`, the caller's `sessionKey` as `childSessionKey`, the message as `task`, `expectsCompletionMessage: false`, `spawnMode: "run"`, `cleanup: "keep"`, and the main-session alias from cfg as `requesterSessionKey`.
- Plugin scope `pluginId` flows through as a `plugin:<id>` registry label so operators can attribute the run.
- When no gateway context is available (no scope, no fallback), the dispatch throws as before and we do not register a half-baked record.
- A registry exception does not break the public SDK contract — `runtime.run` still resolves with `{ runId }`.
- **New (P1 follow-up):** `run()` -> `waitForRun()` -> `getSessionMessages()` reads the transcript back successfully, then explicit `deleteSession()` makes the transcript unreachable. Regression test for the ClawSweeper finding on auto-delete-after-completion.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Before the original patch: plugin `api.on("subagent_ended", handler)` handlers never fired for runs started through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`. Before the follow-up commit on this PR: any plugin that called `getSessionMessages()` after `waitForRun()` would have raced the auto-delete and lost the transcript.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — test added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — `subagent_ended` hooks now fire for plugin-SDK subagent runs, and the child session is preserved for plugin-side readback until the plugin explicitly deletes it. Both behaviors are the intended contract, not regressions.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`. The dispatch still goes through the same gateway `agent` handler with the same `pluginRuntimeOwnerId` plumbing.

What is the highest-risk area?

Double registration on a path that already calls `registerSubagentRun`. Sibling-surface analysis:

- `sessions_spawn` tool (`src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts:434`) calls `callSubagentGateway({ method: "agent", ... })`. That `callSubagentGateway` is a session-scoped helper that does **not** route through the plugin SDK adapter, so my new `registerPluginSubagentRunQuietly` is unreachable from this path. Sessions_spawn continues to be the only registration site.
- `subagent-spawn.ts:1171` (direct spawn) likewise dispatches via `callSubagentGateway`, not via `createGatewaySubagentRuntime`.
- ACP subagent (`sessions-spawn-tool.ts:419` `resolveSubagentSpawnOwnership` + `registerSubagentRun`) and Codex subagent paths both register on their own side and do not flow through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`.
- The only caller of `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run` is the public plugin SDK `api.runtime.subagent.run`. So this fix tracks exactly the set of runs that were missing tracking before, with no overlap.

How is that risk mitigated?

`registerSubagentRun` itself is keyed on `runId` (`src/agents/subagent-registry-run-manager.ts:481-545`) and a duplicate registration with the same `runId` would only overwrite the entry, not double-emit the hook (`emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` guards on `entry.endedHookEmittedAt`). The sibling-surface review above shows the duplicate-registration case is not reachable through current callers.

## Current review state

What is the next action? ClawSweeper re-review of the P1 follow-up, then maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing from the author side. Crabbox/Testbox / real plugin-harness proof can be requested if the source-repro evidence above is not sufficient.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? ClawSweeper P1 finding on auto-delete-after-completion — addressed via the `cleanup: "keep"` default change and the new regression test (see "ClawSweeper P1 follow-up" section above).


---

## Real behavior proof — live hook capture (addresses ClawSweeper P1)

Captured by running a dedicated live-capture test (`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.proof.test.ts`) that drives the **real** stack: `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run()` → `registerPluginSubagentRunQuietly()` → the registry → `completeSubagentRun` → `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`. Only the model/agent execution is stubbed at the lowest gateway seam (`callGateway`); the registry record, hook emission, session store, and `getSessionMessages()`/`deleteSession()` readback are all production code.

Reproduce:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.proof.test.ts --disable-console-intercept
```

Captured output (`qa/proofs/issue-59164-subagent-ended-hook.output.txt`):

```text
=== driving createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run() (real registry path) ===
run() -> runId=plugin-run-live-1
=== registry record (real SubagentRunRecord) ===
{
  "runId": "plugin-run-live-1",
  "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper",
  "requesterSessionKey": "agent:main:main",
  "cleanup": "keep",
  "spawnMode": "run",
  "expectsCompletionMessage": false
}
=== subagent_ended hook fired ===
{
  "event": {
    "targetSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper",
    "targetKind": "subagent",
    "reason": "subagent-complete",
    "sendFarewell": true,
    "runId": "plugin-run-live-1",
    "endedAt": 2000,
    "outcome": "ok"
  },
  "ctx": {
    "runId": "plugin-run-live-1",
    "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper",
    "requesterSessionKey": "agent:main:main"
  }
}
=== getSessionMessages() readback (after subagent_ended, cleanup:keep) ===
{
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "summarize this transcript"
    },
    {
      "role": "assistant",
      "content": "Here is the summary the plugin asked for."
    }
  ]
}
=== explicit deleteSession() ===
deleted agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper; store size now 0
=== getSessionMessages() second readback (after delete) ===
readback after delete -> session agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper not found
=== LIVE PROOF COMPLETE: real subagent_ended fired; session readable then deleted ===
```

What this proves, end to end:
1. `api.runtime.subagent.run()`'s gateway path now creates a real `SubagentRunRecord` with `cleanup: "keep"` (the fix).
2. `subagent_ended` **actually fires** with the correct payload (runId, child/requester keys, `outcome: ok`) — the original bug was that it never fired at all.
3. After completion the child transcript is **still readable** via `getSessionMessages()` (the polish fix — `cleanup:"keep"` did not delete it).
4. Explicit `deleteSession()` then removes it, and a second readback returns `not found` — lifecycle stays under the plugin's control.

Honest scope: this is an in-process live capture on the contributor's machine (macOS), stubbing only the model turn. A full standalone plugin-process harness against a built gateway would be strictly stronger, but the registry + hook + session-retention contract under review is exercised entirely by production code here.

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a purpose-specific memory role-slot contract so memory plugins can compose instead of replacing each other through one global `plugins.slots.memory` selector.

What changes:

- Adds canonical memory role slots: `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`.
- Keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible recall shorthand during an extended migration window.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots`, limited to memory roles plus legacy `memory`.
- Wires role-slot selection through config schema/types/help/docs, plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, validation, doctor repair/preview, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and SDK-facing aliases.
- Preserves the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` path for LosslessClaw and other context-engine plugins.
- Explicitly rejects unsupported per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` overrides, because agent-scoped context-engine semantics are out of scope for this PR.

Why this matters:

- Advanced OpenClaw users are already operating layered memory stacks: factual recall, context compaction, auto-capture, dreaming/consolidation, and inferred user modeling are distinct concerns.
- The previous single memory slot forced useful plugins into workarounds such as context-engine ownership, sidecars, or global replacement of the primary recall plugin.
- This branch makes the config contract match the memory architecture users are trying to run while keeping the migration path conservative.

Intended outcome:

- `memory-core` can remain the factual recall owner while other plugins own capture, compaction, dreaming, or user-model roles.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` copies non-conflicting legacy `plugins.slots.memory` values into `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` but keeps the legacy key in place for plugin compatibility.
- Plugin lifecycle and diagnostics treat role-slot-selected memory plugins as intentionally active across startup, validation, skill publishing, runtime resolution, and cleanup.

Out of scope:

- This does not replace or remove `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; LosslessClaw and other context engines continue to use the existing global `contextEngine` slot.
- This does not add agent-scoped `contextEngine` semantics.
- This does not implement the owner-provenance object shape proposed in #70823.
- This does not require third-party plugins to implement every memory role immediately.

## Linked context

Closes #60572.

Directly related memory-composition requests and prior proposals:

- #38874: supplementary/additive memory plugins alongside the primary memory slot.
- #2306: earlier closed PR proposing stackable memory plugins.
- #2080: earlier request for multiple simultaneous memory plugins and emerging memory architectures.
- #77714: pluggable persistent memory backend demand.

Dreaming and memory-role separation context:

- #85473: future protocol work for making dreaming implementable by non-`memory-core` memory plugins. This PR adds the `memory.dreaming` slot contract but does not implement that full protocol.
- #63067 and #65411: prior work around keeping dreaming behavior available when another plugin owns the legacy memory slot.
- #62275: previous selected-slot dreaming config behavior.

Runtime/diagnostic slot handling context:

- #76567 and #76495: configured memory-slot runtime checks should load/consult the selected memory plugin.
- #78540: doctor/status memory diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` references could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.

Context-engine compatibility context:

- #77063, #77185, #76576, and #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior while rejecting unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` config.

Future API/RFC context:

- #70823: future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps scalar plugin IDs and solves the memory role composition layer first.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Exact-head proof refresh for the multi-slot memory role contract after fixing the legacy-memory doctor sanitizer import at `src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-memory-slot.ts`. The refresh covers recall-default cleanup, legacy `plugins.slots.memory` doctor compatibility, canonical `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]`, per-agent memory role routing, context-engine compatibility preservation, packaging/install upgrade behavior, and installed runtime diagnostics.
- Current PR head tested and pushed: `c9553718a65697ecb75ffb64f37245856fca0eb7` (`fix(memory): correct legacy memory doctor log sanitizer import`). This supersedes the prior public PR head `da7ea0f3d2bd240ec4e9cefcd512e0675489f9fb`.
- Real environment tested: Orion / Nano-01, `Linux Nano-01 5.15.185-tegra ... aarch64`, checkout `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-88504`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, baseline global OpenClaw `2026.5.28 (bb771d0)`.
- Artifact path: `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-88504/.artifacts/proof-refresh-c9553718-20260531T152657Z`.
- Redacted logs: `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-88504/.artifacts/proof-refresh-c9553718-20260531T152657Z/redacted`.
- Installed candidate package: `.artifacts/proof-refresh-c9553718-20260531T152657Z/package/openclaw-2026.5.30.tgz`.
- Installed candidate version after upgrade: `OpenClaw 2026.5.30 (c955371)`.

- Observed result after fix: The exact-head Orion proof validates the fixed import, strict build path, role-slot config behavior, installed package upgrade over OpenClaw `2026.5.28`, installed config/status/plugin/model diagnostics, `doctor --fix` after host migrations, five-agent config validation, role-slot resolution across global and per-agent memory roles, and direct Honcho service health.
- What was not fully green: Four full live agent-turn probes timed out after plugin/tool loading under the GitHub Copilot model path. Honcho itself was directly healthy, and the main live turn returned `PR88504_main_OK`. The timeout is recorded as a live proof limitation rather than reused as positive evidence.

## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` now keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## Verification

Current PR branch head:

- `c9553718a65697ecb75ffb64f37245856fca0eb7` (`fix(memory): correct legacy memory doctor log sanitizer import`)
- Branch: `kklouzal:feat/multi-slot-memory-architecture`
- Base target: `openclaw/openclaw:main`
- Prior head superseded by this proof refresh: `da7ea0f3d2bd240ec4e9cefcd512e0675489f9fb`

Exact-head Orion / Nano-01 proof artifact:

- Baseline installed OpenClaw before upgrade: `OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (bb771d0)`.
- Installed candidate after package upgrade: `OpenClaw 2026.5.30 (c955371)`.

Exact-head local proof gates:

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`: passed.
- `corepack pnpm build:strict-smoke`: passed on rerun with pnpm shim available after initial PATH-only failure.
- Focused memory/config/plugin/status/extension suite: passed across the logged shards, including legacy-memory doctor, loader, config validation, config state, effective plugin ids, active-memory, and memory-wiki bridge coverage.
- Plugin memory suite: passed.
- Command/config memory suite: passed.
- Active-memory extension suite: passed.
- Memory-wiki bridge suite: passed.
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:prod`: passed.

- Honcho direct health probe: passed at `127.0.0.1:8000/health` with `{"status":"ok"}`.

Live proof limitation:

- The `main` live turn completed with `PR88504_main_OK`.
- Four memory-role-heavy live turns timed out under GitHub Copilot/Honcho tool load with `llm-idle-timeout` lines. They are not claimed as positive live-turn proof.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a default-off Telegram interleaved progress mode, shared commentary progress helpers, Telegram config/docs/UI metadata, and regression coverage for reasoning/tool progress rendering.

PR surface: Source +628, Tests +865, Docs +3, Generated 0. Total +1496 across 16 files.

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- A rolling timer (20s cadence) shows elapsed time + a wall-clock stamp while running, so a stalled lane is visible at a glance; cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane. Tagged payloads (`<think>reason</think>answer`) keep only the in-tag reasoning, so answer prose after `</think>` never lands in the lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **Rate-limit-aware cadence** — the rolling "still running" timer ticks every 20s (not every few seconds) so N concurrently-streaming Thinking messages stay under Telegram's per-chat edit rate and don't draw 429s (a 429 drops the edit and stalls the lane); the line carries a wall-clock stamp so a genuine stall is obvious
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Prerequisite

- **#80046** (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args) — **merged 2026-05-29**; supplies the `onToolStart` lifecycle events the tool lines render. This branch should be rebased onto current `main` (which now contains it) before merge.

The renderer otherwise consumes only existing callbacks and is default-off, so it depends on no unmerged work. (On the standard `claude-cli` backend, CLI-*native* reasoning text additionally benefits from #85381's `thinking_delta`; without it the lane simply shows tool/event lines. The interactive backend restores thinking via its proxy, so reasoning appears regardless. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected — this is not a hard dependency.)

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard + wall-clock timer stamp, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append, timer-interval constant)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; 20s rolling-timer cadence; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (extract only in-`<think>` reasoning, dropping answer prose after `</think>`, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle + wall-clock stamp, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, tagged-reasoning split (answer after `</think>` dropped; streaming/code cases), defensive final-answer strip
- [x] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Verification

Local checks at the current head:

```
pnpm tsgo:extensions                                                 # typecheck — clean
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts   # lint — clean
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts \
               extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.test.ts   # all green
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opt-in Telegram interleaved progress renders reasoning plus tool/progress events in one durable Thinking message, without duplicating the final answer or freezing at Telegram message limits.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw Telegram gateway against Telegram, default-off feature explicitly enabled, two concurrent Telegram threads in one chat.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt and ran the gateway from the PR head, enabled channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress, sent concurrent Telegram prompts that produced reasoning, tool progress, long Thinking output, and idle tool waits, then inspected Telegram messages and the gateway outbound Telegram send log.

Evidence after fix: Screenshot embedded below plus redacted outbound Telegram send-log metrics copied below: continuation msg=34783 -> msg=34786, final answer absent from every Thinking edit, zero bare Thinking sends, 20s timer edits at 04:20:15 -> 04:20:35 -> 04:20:55, and 99 outbound calls with 0 failures.

Observed result after fix: Thinking lane showed reasoning plus tool line, rolled into a continuation before the cap, sent the polished answer once as its own message, cleared empty Thinking output, and avoided Telegram 429s under the two-thread concurrent run.

What was not tested: No broad channel matrix or release package lane in this PR; focused local proof covers the Telegram renderer helpers, schema, lint, and extension typecheck.

## Live proof (current head)

Captured on the current head with the default-off feature enabled, over a **two-thread concurrent** session. The screenshot shows the visible lane; the rest is verified from the gateway's **outbound Telegram send log** — every `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` the bot issues — which shows message-level behavior a screenshot can't (message ids, edit cadence, final-state content, timing). Wire-level facts below are message-level metrics (ids, lengths, ok/timing) and contain no command/path/host content.

**Normal interleaving — reasoning + tool-use line in the Thinking lane, with the rolling timer:**

![interleaved lane](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/66da4d16b5a9467a82a65134976bc66aa956519c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-30-toolline.png)

**Wire-level verification (gateway send log, current head, one two-thread concurrent session):**

- **Long-message continuation** — `msg=34783` grew to the 4000-char cap and rolled into a fresh continuation `msg=34786` (carrying the next tool line) instead of freezing; clean seam, no garble.
- **No duplicate final answer** — on that long thinking-heavy turn the final answer was delivered once as its own message and is **absent from every Thinking edit** of `34783`/`34786`.
- **No empty Thinking** — **zero** bare-`Thinking`-header sends across the session.
- **20s rolling-timer cadence (rate-limit relief)** — while idling on a blocking tool, the "still running" line re-edited at **exactly 20s intervals** (04:20:15 → 04:20:35 → 04:20:55, body static at 3764 chars), keeping concurrent Thinking messages under Telegram's edit rate. The wall-clock stamp exposes any genuine stall.
- **No rate-limiting** — **99** outbound calls across the two concurrent threads, **0** failures (no `429`s, no oversized-preview stop) — the exact concurrency that previously drew 429s.

target.title: refactor: clean up ACP translator and manager tests
target.body:
## Summary
- Extract ACP translator session-list and replay helpers with focused tests, and split the large translator bridge coverage into targeted files.
- Extract ACP manager backend failover decisions into a focused helper with tests.
- Split the monolithic ACP manager test coverage into shared harness plus focused failover, runtime-config, runtime-handle, and turn-result suites.
- Move ACP manager task-state test setup out of control-plane helpers so task registry boundary tests stay clean.
- Split the gateway runtime-state CI shard into smaller gateway-server shards so one remote file cannot stall the lane for 15 minutes.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/tasks/task-boundaries.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-results.test.ts test/scripts/ci-node-test-plan.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm test src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.runtime-handles.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-results.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.runtime-config.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.failover.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.backend-failover.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm test src/acp/translator.session-rate-limit.test.ts src/acp/translator.final-snapshots.test.ts src/acp/translator.prompt-size.test.ts src/acp/translator.session-config.test.ts src/acp/translator.session-setup.test.ts src/acp/translator.session-snapshot.test.ts src/acp/translator.tool-streaming.test.ts src/acp/translator.session-list.test.ts src/acp/translator.replay.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- exact old remote runtime-state shard command via `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=... pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts`
- `pnpm deadcode:unused-files`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm lint:core`
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` clean, no accepted/actionable findings after the latest rebase

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: internal ACP refactor only; runtime/session behavior intended unchanged while ownership and tests are split into smaller surfaces. Also splits one slow/stuck CI shard without dropping coverage.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout, Node/pnpm repo wrappers.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused ACP manager tests, focused ACP translator tests, task boundary regression test, CI node-plan tooling test, old gateway runtime-state shard repro, unused-file deadcode check, test typecheck, core/prod typecheck, core lint, extension package-boundary compile, Codex autoreview.
Evidence after fix: all listed local commands passed; PR CI rerun is pending after the latest force-push.
Observed result after fix: ACP manager and translator focused suites pass after the helper/test splits; task registry boundary test stays clean; gateway runtime-state CI lane is split while preserving exact file coverage; autoreview reports no actionable findings.
What was not tested: full `pnpm check:changed` locally because local Crabbox is 0.21.0 and Testbox requires >=0.22.0; PR CI is the broad proof source.

target.title: feat(gateway): support Tailscale Serve service names
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an optional `gateway.tailscale.serviceName` config field for Tailscale Serve.
- Passes the configured Service name through to `tailscale serve --service=<name>` while preserving the existing device-hostname behavior when unset.
- Uses a scoped Serve cleanup command for named Services so `resetOnExit` does not reset unrelated Serve config.
- Documents the new config option in the Tailscale and gateway configuration docs.

## Linked context

Closes #88629.

Related: Tailscale's current Serve CLI exposes `--service=<name>` for Services, with `--bg` and `--yes` still available on the same command.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw had no `gateway.tailscale.serviceName` config path and always configured Serve against the node hostname with `tailscale serve --bg --yes <port>`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw contributor checkout on Linux with Tailscale CLI `1.98.3` installed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `tailscale version`
  - `tailscale serve --help > /tmp/tailscale-serve-help.txt 2>&1`
  - `rg -n -- '--service|--bg|--yes|USAGE' /tmp/tailscale-serve-help.txt`
  - focused Vitest commands listed below
- Evidence after fix: the local Tailscale help output includes `--service value`, `--bg`, and `--yes`; the new unit coverage asserts the exact generated command arguments are `tailscale serve --service=svc:openclaw --bg --yes 3000` and cleanup uses `tailscale serve --service=svc:openclaw off`.
- Observed result after fix: focused tests pass and the unchanged path still calls `enableTailscaleServe(18789)` when `serviceName` is unset.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live `tailscale serve --service=...` command against a tailnet because that would mutate real Tailscale Serve/Service state from a contributor environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proves config propagation and CLI argument generation, not a live Service advertisement in a real tailnet.
- Before evidence: current `enableTailscaleServe()` accepted only `port` and emitted `['serve', '--bg', '--yes', '<port>']`; `GatewayTailscaleConfig` had only `mode`, `resetOnExit`, and `preserveFunnel`.

## Tests and validation

- `corepack pnpm test src/infra/tailscale.test.ts src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts src/gateway/startup-auth.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed.
- `corepack pnpm test src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed.
- `corepack pnpm test src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed.
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 ...` completed on the touched TypeScript files.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, only for users who opt into `gateway.tailscale.serviceName`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Yes, the config schema accepts one new optional field. Existing config remains unchanged.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? Yes, Tailscale Serve command construction changes when the new field is set.

What is the highest-risk area? Incorrectly resetting or overwriting unrelated Tailscale Serve state. Mitigation: the existing no-service path still uses the prior `serve reset` behavior, while the named-Service path uses a scoped `tailscale serve --service=<name> off` cleanup.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review. Waiting on CI and any maintainer preference about whether this config field should also get a CLI flag in a follow-up.

target.title: fix(update): preserve protected Codex routes
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve legacy `openai-codex/*` and `codex/*` protected model/runtime routes during non-interactive doctor repair instead of silently rewriting them to `openai/*`.
- Add update-time protected-route audit/rollback for core update and post-core finalization: if a protected Codex route is rewritten or deleted, restore the pre-update config and fail closed with `protected-route-drift`.
- Mark cross-provider/auth-route-changing fallbacks in fallback telemetry and keep route-changing fallback notices visible even when upstream verbose fallback output is off.
- Document the interactive-vs-non-interactive doctor behavior so operators know when routes are migrated versus preserved.
- Keep interactive `openclaw doctor --fix` migration behavior unchanged.

## Evidence

This is based on the live incident captured in #80628 and comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80628#issuecomment-4585716866.

Observed on OpenClaw 2026.5.28:

- `openclaw doctor` touched `/root/.openclaw/openclaw.json` at `2026-05-31T04:29:14Z`.
- A previously working Codex setup had model refs rewritten from a Codex route to OpenAI-provider refs across `agents.defaults.model.primary` and specialist-agent `model.primary` / `fallbacks` entries.
- The next isolated `News Digest` cron run failed before reply under the Codex harness with:

```text
Requested agent harness "codex" does not support openai/gpt-5.5 (provider is not one of: codex).
```

Local remediation was to patch the config back to Codex model refs, remove `openai/gpt-5.5` from the allowed agent model sets, and let the gateway hot-reload. After remediation, `openclaw models status --json` reported `defaultModel` and `resolvedDefault` as `codex/gpt-5.5` with allowed `codex/gpt-5.5` and `xai/grok-4.3`.

This PR now covers the full failure mode from #80628: doctor preservation, update audit/rollback, missing protected-route deletion detection, and visibility for cross-provider fallback transitions.

Refs #80628, #88450, #88470.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Non-interactive doctor repair and update/finalize flows no longer silently rewrite or delete protected Codex route model/runtime refs.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2, isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; no production config or credentials.
- Exact steps or command run after the patch: Created a temp config containing `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, then ran `HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive --fix`. Then created an isolated pre-update config with protected Codex primary, fallback, and model-scoped runtime routes; created a post-update config where primary/fallback were rewritten to `openai/*` and the Codex runtime route was deleted; then ran `HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/.openclaw/openclaw.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE=1 OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_CHANNEL=stable OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_SOURCE_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/before.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_RESULT_PATH="$tmpdir/post-core-result.json" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw update --json --no-restart`.
- Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the patched branch:

```text
# doctor --non-interactive --fix against isolated config
$ HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive --fix
doctor exit=0
config after doctor:
{
  "model": "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"
}

preservation warning lines:
43:│  - Legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs should be rewritten to            │
49:│  - Preserved legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs during non-interactive  │

last output lines:
Updated config: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  Backup: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
Backup: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
│
└  Doctor complete.

# update/finalize against isolated before/after configs
$ HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/.openclaw/openclaw.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE=1 OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_CHANNEL=stable OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_SOURCE_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/before.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_RESULT_PATH="$tmpdir/post-core-result.json" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw update --json --no-restart
exit=1
stdout:
{
  "status": "error",
  "mode": "unknown",
  "root": "/path/to/openclaw",
  "reason": "protected-route-drift",
  "steps": [],
  "durationMs": 0
}

post-core-result.json:
{
  "status": "error",
  "reason": "protected-route-drift",
  "changed": false,
  "warnings": [
    {
      "reason": "protected-route-drift",
      "message": "Protected model/runtime routes changed during update finalization; restored the pre-update config and stopped the update. agents.defaults.model.fallbacks.0: codex/gpt-5.4 -> openai/gpt-5.4; agents.defaults.model.primary: codex/gpt-5.5 -> openai/gpt-5.5; agents.defaults.models.codex/gpt-5.5.agentRuntime.id: codex -> <missing>",
      "guidance": ["Review the config change source, then rerun openclaw update."]
    }
  ],
  "sync": {
    "changed": false,
    "errors": [
      "agents.defaults.model.fallbacks.0: codex/gpt-5.4 -> openai/gpt-5.4",
      "agents.defaults.model.primary: codex/gpt-5.5 -> openai/gpt-5.5",
      "agents.defaults.models.codex/gpt-5.5.agentRuntime.id: codex -> <missing>"
    ]
  }
}

config after finalize:
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "model": {
        "primary": "codex/gpt-5.5",
        "fallbacks": ["codex/gpt-5.4"]
      },
      "models": {
        "codex/gpt-5.5": {
          "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex" }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "meta": {
    "lastTouchedVersion": "2026.5.31",
    "lastTouchedAt": "<redacted timestamp>"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: Doctor completed and wrote the isolated config while leaving the protected model route as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; update/finalize exited non-zero with `protected-route-drift`, wrote the post-core result file with both rewritten routes and the deleted runtime route, restored the pre-update Codex config, and did not continue plugin update/finalization.
- What was not tested: A live production gateway restart on the patched branch was not run; proof uses isolated temp config paths only.

## Tests

Latest validation on head `51390655b51e` after rebasing onto `origin/main` `5b310a7b27f`:

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm deadcode:dependencies && pnpm deadcode:unused-files && pnpm deadcode:report:ci:ts-unused`
- `env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts` — 159 tests passed
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/agents/compaction-planning-worker.test.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` — 130 tests passed

target.title: fix: preserve explicit provider in resolveConfiguredProviderFallback
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88710

`resolveConfiguredProviderFallback` currently replaces **both** the provider and model with the first available provider's first model when the default provider is not configured. This produces incorrect refs like `ollama/kimi-k2.6:cloud` when the user configured `openai/gpt-5.5`.

## Changes

Three behavioral changes in `src/agents/configured-provider-fallback.ts`:

1. **Model-first lookup**: When `defaultModel` is specified, search all configured providers for one that carries that exact model ID. If found, return that provider with the same model — switching only the provider, preserving the model.

2. **Unknown provider → `null`**: When the default provider is not a configured key at all (e.g., `"openai"` when only `"openai-codex"` exists), return `null` so the caller preserves the user's explicitly-specified `provider/model` pair.

3. **Existing fallback preserved**: Only fall back to the first available provider's first model when the default provider IS configured but doesn't carry the model and no other provider has it either.

## Test Evidence

**Before fix:**
```
resolveConfiguredProviderFallback({ cfg, defaultProvider: "openai", defaultModel: "gpt-5.5" })
→ { provider: "ollama", model: "kimi-k2.6:cloud" }  // BUG: wrong provider AND model
```

**After fix:**
```
resolveConfiguredProviderFallback({ cfg, defaultProvider: "openai", defaultModel: "gpt-5.5" })
→ null  // Caller preserves openai/gpt-5.5
```

**Production log before fix:**
```
[agent/embedded] embedded run agent end: isError=true model=gpt-5.5 provider=ollama error=404 {"error": "model 'gpt-5.5' not found"}
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=ollama/gpt-5.5 candidate=ollama/gpt-5.5 reason=model_not_found next=none
```

The model `gpt-5.5` was incorrectly routed to the ollama API endpoint, which doesn't have it.

## Caller Safety

All callers handle `null` gracefully:
- `model-selection-shared.ts:864` → falls back to `{ provider: defaultProvider, model: defaultModel }`
- `codex-route-warnings.ts:438` → falls back to `${DEFAULT_PROVIDER}/${DEFAULT_MODEL}`
- `status.summary.runtime.ts:83` → falls back to `{ provider: defaultProvider, model: defaultModel }`

target.title: fix(infra): bridge WSL clipboard through shell
target.body:
## Summary

- route WSL2 clipboard copies through a static `/bin/sh -c` bridge to `/mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe`
- use `exec` so the clipboard process remains the timeout-owned child while dashboard/token URLs stay on stdin instead of shell argv
- avoid Bash login/profile startup and preserve the existing macOS, Linux, native Windows, PowerShell, and non-WSL2 fallback order
- tighten the regression test so token substrings cannot hide inside argv elements

Fixes #88080.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clipboard.test.ts src/infra/wsl.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.test.ts` - passed on final head, 34 focused tests
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean on final head, no accepted/actionable findings
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --label wsl-clipboard-88365-check-changed-final --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --stop-after always --shell -- 'pnpm check:changed'` - passed on AWS Crabbox, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_78e26498569a`, run `run_0941fd6bfbce`
- `crabbox run --provider aws --target windows --windows-mode wsl2 --no-hydrate --no-sync --label wsl-clipboard-88365-exec-readback --idle-timeout 30m --ttl 60m --timing-json --stop-after always --script-stdin` - passed on AWS Windows/WSL2 Crabbox, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_8a49bbda915d`, run `run_5500c97a427e`; generated probe wrote through `/bin/sh -c 'exec /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and matched `Get-Clipboard`
- `crabbox run --provider aws --target windows --windows-mode normal --no-hydrate --no-sync --label native-windows-clipboard-88365-final-head --idle-timeout 30m --ttl 60m --timing-json --stop-after always --script-stdin` - passed on AWS native Windows Crabbox, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_0c8c10e18c7f`, run `run_47278979324e`; generated probe wrote through direct `%SystemRoot%\System32\clip.exe` and matched `Get-Clipboard`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WSL2 dashboard clipboard copies no longer rely on direct non-shell `clip.exe` argv execution or a Bash login shell. The WSL2 path uses `/bin/sh -c 'exec /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and passes the copied dashboard URL through stdin, keeping the token out of argv while keeping timeout ownership on the clipboard process.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Windows/WSL2 host for shell-bridge clipboard write/readback; AWS Crabbox native Windows host for direct Windows clipboard write/readback; AWS Crabbox Linux for `pnpm check:changed`; focused local Vitest/format/lint/diff-check; branch autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest, oxfmt, touched-file oxlint, diff-check, branch autoreview, AWS Linux `pnpm check:changed`, AWS Windows/WSL2 clipboard readback, and AWS native Windows clipboard readback commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: the Windows/WSL2 run wrote a generated probe string through `/bin/sh -c 'exec /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and read it back with Windows PowerShell `Get-Clipboard`; expected and actual values matched exactly. The native Windows run wrote a generated probe through direct `%SystemRoot%\System32\clip.exe` and read it back with `Get-Clipboard`; expected and actual values matched. The WSL2 regression test asserts copied values stay on stdin rather than argv, and the non-WSL fallback-order test keeps the existing `pbcopy`, `xclip`, `wl-copy`, `clip.exe`, and PowerShell order.

Observed result after fix: Windows/WSL2 clipboard readback passed on commit `0720cb12084c9916d22dbe4868c7e124c6186cf1`; native Windows clipboard readback passed; AWS `pnpm check:changed` passed; focused tests, touched-file format/lint, diff-check, and autoreview passed.

What was not tested: `openclaw dashboard --no-open` end-to-end with a real token URL on a user workstation. The runner proof directly exercised the WSL2 clipboard bridge that dashboard calls, using non-secret generated probe values.

target.title: [codex] fix(telegram): sync native commands to private and group scopes [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

Telegram native slash-command menus are now synced and cleared for default, private-chat, and group-chat scopes. This prevents private chats from retaining stale command menus when the bot updates or disables native commands.

AI-assisted by Codex GPT-5.5.

## What Changed

- Added `all_private_chats` to the shared Telegram command-menu scope list.
- Added private-chat cleanup to the explicit native-disabled command cleanup path.
- Kept command construction, authorization, transport behavior, and config unchanged.
- Kept unchanged-menu skip logging on the verbose-only path, matching the pre-existing rate-limit guard behavior.
- Added focused regression coverage for scope ordering, empty-menu cleanup, cache skips, localized command variants, retry behavior, native-disabled cleanup, and unchanged-menu logging.

## Scope Boundaries

- No new permissions or secrets.
- No dependency, package, or CI changes.
- No change to the commands themselves; only the Telegram Bot API scopes used to set and clear native command menus changed.

## Verification

Verified on rebased head `6798383d1ae9bace7d6cce5a4552f47b13401e58` against base `84b025eb622176483ebb1615a88059a4368b3775`.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts` passed: 1 file, 26 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts` passed: 2 files, 51 tests.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` produced no output.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram native command menu sync now deletes and sets commands for `default`, `all_private_chats`, and `all_group_chats`; empty-menu cleanup also clears the private-chat scope.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 24 runtime against current PR head `6798383d1ae9bace7d6cce5a4552f47b13401e58`, executing the production `syncTelegramMenuCommands` path with a Bot API capture adapter for `deleteMyCommands` and `setMyCommands` calls.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** From the PR worktree, ran a `pnpm exec tsx` probe that imported `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-command-menu.ts`, invoked `syncTelegramMenuCommands` once with a non-empty command list and once with an empty command list, and captured the emitted Telegram Bot API method/options sequence.
- **Evidence after fix:** Current-head terminal output from the Bot API call-sequence probe:

```json
{
  "head": "6798383d1ae9bace7d6cce5a4552f47b13401e58",
  "base": "84b025eb622176483ebb1615a88059a4368b3775",
  "registrationCalls": [
    { "method": "deleteMyCommands", "scope": "default" },
    { "method": "deleteMyCommands", "scope": "all_private_chats" },
    { "method": "deleteMyCommands", "scope": "all_group_chats" },
    { "method": "setMyCommands", "scope": "default", "commandCount": 1, "languageCode": null },
    { "method": "setMyCommands", "scope": "all_private_chats", "commandCount": 1, "languageCode": null },
    { "method": "setMyCommands", "scope": "all_group_chats", "commandCount": 1, "languageCode": null }
  ],
  "cleanupCalls": [
    { "method": "deleteMyCommands", "scope": "default" },
    { "method": "deleteMyCommands", "scope": "all_private_chats" },
    { "method": "deleteMyCommands", "scope": "all_group_chats" }
  ],
  "runtimeLogs": [],
  "runtimeErrors": []
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The production sync path emitted delete-then-set calls for default, private-chat, and group-chat scopes, and the empty-menu path emitted deletes for all three scopes without setting any commands.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Telegram Bot API token or Telegram Desktop visual proof in this workspace. That still needs Mantis/maintainer proof if the project requires remote Telegram UI confirmation.

## Review Status

- No inline review threads remain open.
- The latest ClawSweeper review finding about unchanged-menu skip logging was addressed in `6798383d1ae9bace7d6cce5a4552f47b13401e58`.
- ClawSweeper may still ask for live Telegram proof or an explicit maintainer proof override before merge.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): add memory flush failure tracking with retry exhaustion
target.body:
## Summary

Fix memory flush failures permanently wedging sessions by adding per-session failure tracking, bounded retry, and a non-destructive retry-exhaustion path.

When a session exceeds the `memoryFlush` threshold, OpenClaw starts an embedded agent turn to create a handoff summary before compacting history. If that provider call fails, current `main` only logs/surfaces the error and persists no failure state. The transcript stays over threshold, so each later user message can retry the same failing flush and never reach the normal reply path.

This PR records consecutive non-abort memory flush failures and, after three failures in the same compaction cycle, marks that cycle as exhausted so the next normal reply can proceed. No transcript content is truncated or deleted by this PR.

Partially addresses #85645.

## Changes

- Add `memoryFlushFailureCount`, `memoryFlushLastFailedAt`, and `memoryFlushLastFailureError` to `SessionEntry`.
- Reserve the new session fields from plugin session-entry slots.
- On non-abort flush failure, persist failure metadata and emit `memory_flush_failed` lifecycle events.
- On successful flush, clear failure metadata.
- After 3 consecutive failures, persist `memoryFlushCompactionCount` for the current cycle, emit `memory_flush_exhausted`, and show a visible warning that memory flush is being skipped for this cycle.
- Keep abort errors retryable without recording failure metadata.
- Add regression coverage for failure tracking, abort exclusion, success cleanup, retry-before-exhaustion, and exhaustion marking.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: A memoryFlush provider failure can leave the session over threshold with no durable failure state, causing repeated flush failure before normal replies can proceed.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS with the repository Node/Vitest harness; PR author also reported Docker `node:24-bookworm` coverage before maintainer fixup.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
git diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts: 40 tests passed
pnpm check:test-types: no longer fails on agent-runner-memory.test.ts; local checkout then hit unrelated current-main doctor migration errors in src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrations.channels.ts
autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
git diff --check: clean
```

- **Observed result after fix**: Non-abort flush failures increment durable session failure metadata and emit lifecycle events. Before the exhaustion threshold, the next message retries memory flush. At the threshold, the current compaction cycle is marked exhausted so the normal reply path can proceed without deleting transcript history; a later compaction cycle can retry memory flush.
- **What was not tested**: Live end-to-end provider crash with a real OpenClaw Gateway instance. The failure path is covered with `runWithModelFallback` rejection against real session-store JSON on disk.

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm check:test-types` to confirm the PR-owned type error is gone; unrelated current-main doctor migration type errors remain locally
- [x] `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- [x] `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(feishu): gate bitable tools by tools config
target.body:
## Summary

- add `channels.feishu.tools.bitable` and `channels.feishu.tools.base` schema/type/default support
- gate `feishu_bitable_*` registration through Feishu tools config
- make top-level `tools.bitable=false` / `tools.base=false` a hard gate over account-level enables
- preserve account-level tools inheritance via nested tools merge
- add regression coverage for top-level/account-level disable and mixed-account routing

## Why

`feishu_bitable_*` tools were registered even when Feishu tool gating disabled other tool families. That made `channels.feishu.tools` incomplete and could keep large/sensitive Bitable tool schemas model-visible despite config intent.

## Validation

The Feishu docs list both the canonical `bitable` gate and legacy `base` alias.
