target.title: fix(ui): preserve chat sends across reconnect
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep in-flight Webchat sends tied to the current send attempt so stale pre-reconnect completions cannot remove or duplicate replayed queued messages.
- Mark stopped same-scope Gateway requests as retryable, while preventing gateway-scope changes from replaying old in-flight sends.
- Hide and ignore remove actions for messages already being sent.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (132 tests passed after rebase)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts ui/src/ui/gateway.ts ui/src/ui/chat/chat-queue.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-no-raw-window-open.mjs`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- `git diff --check`

`node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check` currently fails on latest `upstream/main` due to unrelated raw-copy baseline drift in `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts` from upstream commit `1e3542bbe7a986ebd920a827aafe47f076f381d5`; this branch does not touch that file.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Webchat queued sends survive same-gateway reconnect races (hello before stale close, ACK loss during in-flight send) without stale old `chat.send` completions removing the replayed send; gateway-scope changes do not mark old pending sends retryable.

Real environment tested: Actual local OpenClaw Gateway from this branch, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, auth disabled on loopback port `19123`, and Google Chrome loading the real Control UI at `http://127.0.0.1:19123/chat`; plus mocked-Gateway Playwright E2E harness and focused Vitest reconnect regressions on this branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
# Live Gateway + Chrome send smoke (baseline connectivity)
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-webchat-proof.HmxgCQ/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-webchat-proof.HmxgCQ/state/openclaw.json \
pnpm openclaw gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --auth none --bind loopback --port 19123 --force --verbose

node --input-type=module <local Chrome WebChat proof script>

# Reconnect/replay regressions exercised on this branch
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts \
  -t "replays in-flight|stopped gateway client|ignores stale in-flight|retryablePending|gateway-scope|pre-ack socket close"
```

Evidence after fix:

Live Gateway + Chrome smoke (unchanged baseline):

```text
2026-05-31T10:51:35.605+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 2.4s)
2026-05-31T10:51:35.628+08:00 [gateway] ready

REAL_WEBCHAT_PROOF
url=http://127.0.0.1:19123/chat
send.method=chat.send
send.sessionKey=agent:dev:main
send.deliver=false
send.message=live proof webchat send queue smoke
send.idempotencyKey=f4aae40c-b566-4b26-a3b7-06a2ab3f1189
ack.ok=true
screenshot=/tmp/openclaw-webchat-proof.HmxgCQ/live-webchat-send.png
```

Reconnect/replay regressions (2026-05-31 local rerun on `ef8fad43e38`):

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts \
  -t "replays in-flight|stopped gateway client|ignores stale in-flight|retryablePending|gateway-scope|pre-ack socket close"

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  6 passed | 120 skipped (126)
```

Cases covered by those 6 tests:
- `replays in-flight queued chat sends when hello arrives before stale close` — fast reconnect replay with same `idempotencyKey`
- `keeps a stopped gateway client error recoverable with the same run id` — `retryablePending` stop path
- `ignores stale in-flight send completion after reconnect replay starts/succeeds` — stale completion cannot drop replayed queue item
- `retries reconnectable queued chat sends after reconnect hello` / gateway-scope gating / `pre-ack socket close` recoverable paths

Additional browser-level reconnect proof exists in `ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts` (`retries an ACK-lost send after reconnect with the same idempotency key`): Playwright drives the real Control UI against a mocked Gateway WebSocket, defers the first `chat.send` ACK, closes the socket, and asserts a second `chat.send` reuses the same idempotency key and clears the queue.

Observed result after fix: Same-scope reconnect replay is covered by focused unit regressions plus an existing mocked-Gateway browser E2E case; live Gateway proof confirms the real WebSocket send path still works on this branch. Stale pre-reconnect completions no longer remove replayed sends in the tested race paths.

What was not tested: A manually timed live Gateway reconnect while holding an in-flight deferred `chat.send` ACK (timing-sensitive). `checks-node-agentic-agents-core` failure on `src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts:853` is unrelated to this UI-only diff.

commands.author: SebTardif

target.title: fix(install): harden stdin consumers to prevent pipe corruption in curl | bash
target.body:
## Problem

When invoked via `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, bash reads the script from stdin. Non-interactive subprocesses (npm, openclaw daemon, openclaw plugins) inherit stdin and can consume bytes from the script stream, causing truncated function names and hangs.

PR #82918 attempted to fix this with a top-level pipe guard, but was [closed by steipete](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82918#issuecomment-4568506465) because the detection heuristic (`BASH_SOURCE[0]` + `/dev/stdin` pipe check) was too broad and fired in `bash -c` and other non-piped modes.

## Fix

Follow steipete's guidance: harden the actual stdin consumers instead of adding a global guard.

- **Non-interactive subprocesses** (`npm install`, `openclaw daemon restart`, `openclaw plugins update`, `openclaw dashboard`): redirect stdin from `/dev/null` so they cannot consume script bytes.
- **Interactive commands** (`openclaw onboard` in bootstrap): redirect stdin from `/dev/tty` so the onboarding process gets terminal input instead of the script stream.
- **Wrapper functions** (`run_with_spinner`, `run_quiet_step`): add `< /dev/null` to fallback paths where raw commands run without gum's stdin protection.

Interactive prompts (`prompt_choice`, `gum choose`, `openclaw doctor`, final `exec openclaw onboard`) already use `/dev/tty` and are unchanged.

This approach has no detection heuristics, no re-execution, and no behavior change for direct execution (`bash install.sh`). Each subprocess simply gets the correct stdin for its purpose.

Fixes #73814

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: non-interactive subprocesses in install.sh can steal stdin bytes when the script is piped via `curl | bash`, causing truncated function names and hangs.
- Real environment tested: macOS, bash 3.2 and 5.2, Node v24.2.0, openclaw main at latest.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  # Piped dry-run (simulates curl | bash)
  cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run --install-method npm

  # Direct dry-run (simulates bash install.sh)
  bash scripts/install.sh --dry-run --install-method npm

  # Verify subprocess stdin is isolated
  bash -c 'read -t 0.1 line; echo "got: ${#line} chars"' < /dev/null
  # Output: got: 0 chars
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal output):

  Stdin isolation behavior verified in the terminal:

  ```console
  $ echo "hello from stdin" | bash -c 'read line; echo "got: $line"'
  got: hello from stdin
  $ echo "hello from stdin" | bash -c 'read line < /dev/null; echo "got: $line"'
  got:
  ```

  Without `< /dev/null`, the subprocess consumes "hello from stdin" from the pipe. With `< /dev/null`, the subprocess gets empty stdin and the pipe stream is preserved for the parent script.

  Patched subprocess counts in install.sh:

  ```console
  $ grep -c '< /dev/null' scripts/install.sh
  9
  $ grep -c '< /dev/tty' scripts/install.sh
  3
  ```

  9 non-interactive subprocess calls redirect stdin from `/dev/null` (npm install, daemon restart, plugins update, dashboard, npm config, run_with_spinner fallbacks, run_quiet_step fallback). 3 interactive calls redirect from `/dev/tty` (onboard, which needs terminal input for user prompts).

  Dry-run through pipe completes without truncation:

  ```console
  $ cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run --install-method npm
  Dry run: yes
  ✓ Dry run complete (no changes made)
  $ bash scripts/install.sh --dry-run --install-method npm
  Dry run: yes
  ✓ Dry run complete (no changes made)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: subprocesses receive `/dev/null` as stdin and cannot consume bytes from the script stream during `curl | bash` execution. Interactive commands (`openclaw onboard`) receive `/dev/tty` so user prompts still work. Both piped and direct execution modes complete without truncation or hangs.
- What was not tested: full npm install in piped mode (requires clean environment); WSL2 reproduction of the original truncation from #73814. The fix is mechanical (`< /dev/null` and `< /dev/tty` redirections) and does not depend on platform-specific behavior.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened because two independent delivery paths were active simultaneously:

1. **Draft stream path**: `onPartialReply` → `bot.api.sendMessage()` sent preview text immediately
2. **ACP projector path**: `suppressBlockUserDelivery` blocked ACP block delivery but couldn't reach the upstream draft stream
3. **TTS finalization**: voice note with caption sent after synthesis, then draft text deleted

This PR eliminates the churn by suppressing draft stream creation at source when captioned final TTS will be used.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. onPartialReply fires → draft stream creates sendMessage preview → user sees text
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered → draft text deleted
  Result: user sees text flash then disappear = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan() → willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true
  2. canStreamAnswerDraft = false → NO draft stream created
  3. TTS synthesis runs silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/tts/captioned-final-tts.ts` | **New** shared helper `resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan()` combining channel capability, TTS mode, and TTS status checks |
| `src/tts/captioned-final-tts.test.ts` | **New** 4 unit tests for the shared helper |
| `src/plugin-sdk/tts-runtime.ts` | Re-export `resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan` for extension access |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | Refactored to use shared helper instead of inline TTS logic; removed `channelTtsCapabilitiesRuntimeLoader` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 4 regression tests for deferred settlement, block suppression, abort fallback |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` | Added `isTelegramInboundAudioContext()`, `resolveTelegramSessionTtsAuto()`, and `!willUseCaptionedFinalTts` guard on `canStreamAnswerDraft` |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` | 2 new tests: draft suppression when captioned TTS active, normal streaming when not |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | Block delivery suppression support |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | Delivery coordinator for suppressed text fallback |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 58 passed (58)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/captioned-final-tts.test.ts
 ✓ 4 passed (4)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
 ✓ 96 passed (96)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

$ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
 OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

Total: 170 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode "delete-and-resend churn" — when TTS mode is `final`, users saw a text message flash briefly (2-5 seconds), then it disappeared and a voice note with caption appeared. Root cause: two independent delivery paths (draft stream via `onPartialReply` and ACP block suppression via `suppressBlockUserDelivery`) operated at different layers, so the draft stream was never suppressed.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `e33c474a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`, `messages.tts.auto: "always"`, `channels.telegram.streaming.mode: "block"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/48AAB56AD0D1DC685CB3918F75AEB402)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message ("老板好，好久不见！有什么需要效劳的？") AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption. This is the churn behavior described in #83511: the draft stream sends text via `sendMessage` immediately, then TTS synthesis completes and sends a separate voice note.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Applied patch (commit `e33c474a`) via `npm link`
  2. Restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Sent messages from Telegram to the bot
  4. Observed: voice note with caption appeared directly — no text flash

- **Evidence after fix (live Telegram screenshot)**:

  ![After fix — voice messages with caption, no text churn](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/6937B66CC8A27C33B76FBD54155B542C)

  The screenshot shows two voice messages from the bot, each with text caption in the same bubble. No separate text messages were sent — the text is delivered as caption on the voice note, exactly as intended.

- **Observed result after fix**: Voice notes are delivered as a single message with text caption. No draft text message is created or deleted. The `canStreamAnswerDraft` flag is correctly set to `false` when captioned final TTS is planned, preventing draft stream creation at source. Non-TTS conversations continue to use normal streaming. Other channels (Discord, Slack, etc.) are unaffected.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS behavior (tested in DM only). The `auto=inbound` edge case was verified by unit tests but not live-tested.

Closes #83511


---

## Additional fix: dispatch-from-config text deferral + draft stream suppression (e33c474a)

The original fix addressed only `dispatch-acp.ts` and `bot-message-dispatch.ts`. Live testing revealed two additional issues:

### Issue 1: Missing text deferral in `dispatch-from-config.ts`
The user's agent used the `dispatch-from-config` path (not ACP), which lacked the `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` gate. Block text was delivered before TTS voice synthesis completed, causing split messages.

**Fix**: Added `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` gate in `dispatch-from-config.ts` with:
- Block text accumulation and deferral
- Final payload text merging when resolver returns text
- Deferred text recovery on abort/error paths via `routeReplyToOriginating`
- 8 targeted tests (189 total in dispatch-from-config.test.ts)

### Issue 2: Draft stream (`onPartialReply`) operating independently
The Telegram draft stream sent real-time text previews via `editMessageText` independently from block delivery suppression, causing text to flicker before voice+caption arrived.

**Fix**: Suppress `onPartialReply` when `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` is active:
```typescript
onPartialReply: shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts
  ? undefined
  : wrapProgressCallback(params.replyOptions?.onPartialReply),
```

### Additional files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | Text deferral gate + draft stream suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | 9 new tests for deferral, merge, abort, error, routing, and draft suppression |
| `src/tts/tts-config.ts` | Type definitions for captioned TTS |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability |
| `extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery-text-deliverer.ts` | TTS voice caption merge in lane deliverer |
| `extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts` | 2 new tests for lane deliverer merge path |

### Verified fix (e33c474a)

![After fix — single voice message with caption, no text flicker](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/A1CC95B74A5340F384CAB69EDB482F34.jpg)

### Test results (all passing)

```
dispatch-from-config.test.ts:     189 passed
dispatch-from-config.acp-abort:    16 passed
dispatch-acp.test.ts:              59 passed
lane-delivery.test.ts:             34 passed
Total:                            298 passed
```

target.title: fix(update): preserve protected Codex routes
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve legacy `openai-codex/*` and `codex/*` protected model/runtime routes during non-interactive doctor repair instead of silently rewriting them to `openai/*`.
- Add update-time protected-route audit/rollback for core update and post-core finalization: if a protected Codex route is rewritten or deleted, restore the pre-update config and fail closed with `protected-route-drift`.
- Mark cross-provider/auth-route-changing fallbacks in fallback telemetry and keep route-changing fallback notices visible even when upstream verbose fallback output is off.
- Document the interactive-vs-non-interactive doctor behavior so operators know when routes are migrated versus preserved.
- Keep interactive `openclaw doctor --fix` migration behavior unchanged.

## Evidence

This is based on the live incident captured in #80628 and comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80628#issuecomment-4585716866.

Observed on OpenClaw 2026.5.28:

- `openclaw doctor` touched `/root/.openclaw/openclaw.json` at `2026-05-31T04:29:14Z`.
- A previously working Codex setup had model refs rewritten from a Codex route to OpenAI-provider refs across `agents.defaults.model.primary` and specialist-agent `model.primary` / `fallbacks` entries.
- The next isolated `News Digest` cron run failed before reply under the Codex harness with:

```text
Requested agent harness "codex" does not support openai/gpt-5.5 (provider is not one of: codex).
```

Local remediation was to patch the config back to Codex model refs, remove `openai/gpt-5.5` from the allowed agent model sets, and let the gateway hot-reload. After remediation, `openclaw models status --json` reported `defaultModel` and `resolvedDefault` as `codex/gpt-5.5` with allowed `codex/gpt-5.5` and `xai/grok-4.3`.

This PR now covers the full failure mode from #80628: doctor preservation, update audit/rollback, missing protected-route deletion detection, and visibility for cross-provider fallback transitions.

Refs #80628, #88450, #88470.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Non-interactive doctor repair and update/finalize flows no longer silently rewrite or delete protected Codex route model/runtime refs.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2, isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; no production config or credentials.
- Exact steps or command run after the patch: Created a temp config containing `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, then ran `HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive --fix`. Then created an isolated pre-update config with protected Codex primary, fallback, and model-scoped runtime routes; created a post-update config where primary/fallback were rewritten to `openai/*` and the Codex runtime route was deleted; then ran `HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/.openclaw/openclaw.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE=1 OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_CHANNEL=stable OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_SOURCE_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/before.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_RESULT_PATH="$tmpdir/post-core-result.json" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw update --json --no-restart`.
- Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the patched branch:

```text
# doctor --non-interactive --fix against isolated config
$ HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive --fix
doctor exit=0
config after doctor:
{
  "model": "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"
}

preservation warning lines:
43:│  - Legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs should be rewritten to            │
49:│  - Preserved legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs during non-interactive  │

last output lines:
Updated config: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  Backup: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
Backup: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
│
└  Doctor complete.

# update/finalize against isolated before/after configs
$ HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/.openclaw/openclaw.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE=1 OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_CHANNEL=stable OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_SOURCE_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/before.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_RESULT_PATH="$tmpdir/post-core-result.json" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw update --json --no-restart
exit=1
stdout:
{
  "status": "error",
  "mode": "unknown",
  "root": "/path/to/openclaw",
  "reason": "protected-route-drift",
  "steps": [],
  "durationMs": 0
}

post-core-result.json:
{
  "status": "error",
  "reason": "protected-route-drift",
  "changed": false,
  "warnings": [
    {
      "reason": "protected-route-drift",
      "message": "Protected model/runtime routes changed during update finalization; restored the pre-update config and stopped the update. agents.defaults.model.fallbacks.0: codex/gpt-5.4 -> openai/gpt-5.4; agents.defaults.model.primary: codex/gpt-5.5 -> openai/gpt-5.5; agents.defaults.models.codex/gpt-5.5.agentRuntime.id: codex -> <missing>",
      "guidance": ["Review the config change source, then rerun openclaw update."]
    }
  ],
  "sync": {
    "changed": false,
    "errors": [
      "agents.defaults.model.fallbacks.0: codex/gpt-5.4 -> openai/gpt-5.4",
      "agents.defaults.model.primary: codex/gpt-5.5 -> openai/gpt-5.5",
      "agents.defaults.models.codex/gpt-5.5.agentRuntime.id: codex -> <missing>"
    ]
  }
}

config after finalize:
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "model": {
        "primary": "codex/gpt-5.5",
        "fallbacks": ["codex/gpt-5.4"]
      },
      "models": {
        "codex/gpt-5.5": {
          "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex" }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "meta": {
    "lastTouchedVersion": "2026.5.31",
    "lastTouchedAt": "<redacted timestamp>"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: Doctor completed and wrote the isolated config while leaving the protected model route as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; update/finalize exited non-zero with `protected-route-drift`, wrote the post-core result file with both rewritten routes and the deleted runtime route, restored the pre-update Codex config, and did not continue plugin update/finalization.
- What was not tested: A live production gateway restart on the patched branch was not run; proof uses isolated temp config paths only.

## Tests

Latest validation on head `51390655b51e` after rebasing onto `origin/main` `5b310a7b27f`:

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm deadcode:dependencies && pnpm deadcode:unused-files && pnpm deadcode:report:ci:ts-unused`
- `env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts` — 159 tests passed
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/agents/compaction-planning-worker.test.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` — 130 tests passed

target.title: fix(whatsapp): suppress spurious typing indicator on silent tool-only runs
target.body: # fix(whatsapp): suppress spurious typing indicator on silent tool-only runs ## Summary _This fix was developed with assistance from GitHub Copilot (Claude Sonnet 4.6)._ _Maintainer update: the WhatsApp `suppressTyping` path is scoped to unmentioned message-tool-only group turns, so automatic visible replies and authorized group commands keep their typing indicators._ **What problem does this PR solve?** WhatsApp shows a "typing..." indicator to group members whenever an agent run executes a tool silently — even when the run produces no visible reply. Two distinct causes both contribute to the same false positive: - **Cause 1** (`src/auto-reply/reply/typing-mode.ts`): In message-mode typing, `signalToolStart` unconditionally started the composing loop as soon as a tool call began, before any renderable text had been produced. A run that only ever calls a tool and returns a silent token would flash "typing..." for the entire tool execution window with no message following it. The fix adds a `!hasRenderableText` guard so the indicator stays active during tool calls on runs that already started composing, but stays silent on runs that have not yet produced any text. - **Cause 2** (`extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.ts`): When using Gemini with thinking enabled, the model sometimes emits its reasoning about why it should stay silent as a regular `text` stream part (not tagged `thought: true`), before the silent-reply token. OpenClaw's streaming tap sees these as renderable text deltas, fires composing, and by the time the reply resolver identifies the silent token the indicator has already been sent. **Why does this matter now?** The "typing..." indicator in WhatsApp is highly visible. It appears under the contact name in the chat list and in the conversation header of the group — it's impossible to miss. In group chats where the bot was not mentioned, the overwhelming majority of agent runs are background tool-only runs: passive observation, ambient web search, context collection, and so on. Every one of those runs currently produces a false positive: group members see the bot start "typing," wait, and then nothing arrives. Over time this erodes trust in the agent and makes the bot look broken. **Why WhatsApp specifically, and not Telegram or other channels?** The combination of three factors makes this particularly acute for WhatsApp groups: 1. **Persistence of the composing state.** WhatsApp presence is a push-and-hold model: `sendPresenceUpdate("composing")` stays active until explicitly cleared with `"paused"`. A single false-positive lasts for the full duration of the tool execution (seconds to tens of seconds). Telegram's `sendChatAction` is a fire-and-forget signal that auto-expires in ~5 seconds; it is far less noticeable and corrects itself quickly without any action. 2. **The passive group agent use case is WhatsApp-dominant.** WhatsApp group chats without an @mention are the primary deployment pattern for ambient/background agents (e.g., a bot that silently searches the web on every message but only replies when it has something to say). This pattern is much less common in Telegram groups, where bots are typically explicitly invoked. 3. **Mention semantics.** WhatsApp's `wasMentioned` signal is the natural boundary between "bot was addressed" (typing indicator is appropriate) and "bot is listening silently" (typing indicator is disruptive). This PR uses exactly that boundary for the Cause 2 mitigation. **What is the intended outcome?** - Silent tool-only runs in WhatsApp groups without a mention no longer send any composing presence update. - Runs that produce a visible reply (whether the bot was mentioned or not) are unaffected. - Direct-chat runs and group runs where the bot was mentioned retain the typing indicator as before. **What is intentionally out of scope?** - A complete architectural fix for Cause 2. The root problem — Gemini emitting reasoning as a regular text stream before a silent token — cannot be fully eliminated within the current two-pipeline model (eager streaming tap vs. lazy delivery decision). A complete fix would require collapsing the pipelines or introducing a speculative/rollback composing model. - Changes to direct-chat behavior in WhatsApp. **What does success look like?** A multi-turn Gemini run in a WhatsApp group where the bot is not mentioned, instructed to call a tool silently and not reply, produces no "typing..." indicator at any point. The group sees nothing until (if ever) the bot decides to send a message. **What should reviewers focus on?** - The scoped Cause 2 mitigation: unmentioned message-tool-only group turns suppress composing, while automatic visible replies and authorized group commands keep typing indicators. --- ## Linked context Which issue does this close? No dedicated issue exists for this exact bug. Self-identified from live session logs showing false positive composing on every silent Gemini tool run in WhatsApp groups. Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? - Related commit `da95b58a2a` (closes #450, #447) — "Typing: keep indicators active during tool runs." That commit added unconditional `startTypingLoop()` in `signalToolStart` when typing was not yet active, to fix the indicator dropping out mid-tool-call. The fix was correct for instant/thinking modes but had no mode guard, so message-mode runs that never produced renderable text would also fire composing. Our Cause 1 fix adds the missing `!hasRenderableText` guard — silent runs stay silent, and the indicator still stays alive for any run that already started composing before the tool call. - Related #78963 — "WhatsApp: add listen-only / hooks-only mode for inbound messages without agent runs." The use case described there — passive agents monitoring WhatsApp groups without replying — is exactly the scenario where the spurious typing indicator is most disruptive. This PR does not implement listen-only mode, but it eliminates the most visible symptom for users already running passive group agents today. - Related #87665 — "Discord subagent tasks default to done_only, leaving channel stuck on typing indicator." A sibling bug on Discord: typing indicator fired on a run that produces no message. Not addressed here but shares the same root cause class. Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? Self-identified. --- ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Spurious WhatsApp "typing..." indicator on silent agent tool runs in group chats. - **Real environment tested:** macOS, local OpenClaw gateway (Node 26, pnpm dev), WhatsApp group chat, provider `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` with thinking enabled. Agent config below (obfuscated). - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Sent three consecutive messages into a WhatsApp group: (1) plain message without @mention, (2) @mention with a question, (3) plain message without @mention. All three were received and processed by the running patched gateway. - **Evidence after fix:** Gateway log excerpt and config below; screenshots attached _(before/after to be added)_. - **Observed result after fix:** No "typing..." indicator appeared for messages 1 and 3. A "typing..." indicator appeared normally before the bot's reply to message 2 (the @mention). All three runs called `web_search`. - **What was not tested:** Telegram, Discord, and other channel behavior. The Cause 1 fix is in shared typing-mode code (`src/auto-reply/reply/typing-mode.ts`) and applies to all channels, but only WhatsApp was tested live. The Cause 2 mitigation (`suppressTyping`) is WhatsApp-specific. - **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Cause 2 was only reliably reproduced with a Gemini model with thinking enabled and a system prompt that instructs the model to be silent. Other models were not tested for this specific cause. **Agent and channel config (obfuscated `openclaw.json` excerpt):** ```jsonc { "agents": { "defaults": { "model": { "primary": "google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview" } }, "list": [ { "id": "dev", "identity": { "name": "C3-PO", "theme": "protocol droid", "emoji": "🤖" } } ] }, "channels": { "whatsapp": { "enabled": true, "accounts": { "test": { "groupPolicy": "allowlist", "groupAllowFrom": ["*"], "groups": { "[group-jid]@g.us": { "requireMention": false, "systemPrompt": "For every message, silently call the web_search tool on it. Do not produce any text in your response under any circumstances." } } } } } } } ``` **Messages sent (sender redacted) and bot response:** ``` # Run 1 — no @mention → "[user]: Should I eat a biscuit?" ← (no reply sent — silent run) # Run 2 — @mention → "[user]: @C3-PO should I eat a biscuit?" ← C3-PO: "If you need a quick energy boost and it fits into your diet, a biscuit should be fine! ..." (reply delivered to group) # Run 3 — no @mention → "[user]: Should I drink some water?" ← (no reply sent — silent run) ``` **Before evidence (observed, from session 2026-05-27, run `7477d410`):** ``` 19:10:55 signalTextDelta renderable text detected: "The" ← false positive triggers composing 19:10:55 [TYPING] sendComposing → 120363406415684625@g.us ← visible to group members 19:10:55 Skipping auto-reply: silent token or no text/media returned from resolver 19:10:55 (no WhatsApp message sent) ``` Typing indication for empty respones prior to fix: **After evidence (2026-05-31, three runs in group `120363406415684625@g.us`):** ``` # Run 1 — no @mention (runId d43777cf), web_search, silent 15:34:09 embedded run start 15:34:25 embedded run tool start: tool=web_search 15:34:36 embedded run tool end 15:34:43 embedded run done 15:34:43 Skipping auto-reply: silent token or no text/media returned from resolver ← zero [TYPING] sendComposing entries; group saw no typing indicator ✓ # Run 2 — WITH @mention (runId fde88fe3), web_search, replied 15:34:57 embedded run start 15:34:59 embedded run tool start: tool=web_search 15:34:59 embedded run tool end 15:35:03 embedded run done 15:35:04 Skipping recent outbound WhatsApp echo 3EB00C85BA15C4733E7BDF ← outbound message confirmed; group saw typing indicator before reply ✓ # Run 3 — no @mention (runId f52d483d), web_search, silent 15:35:21 embedded run start 15:35:27 embedded run tool start: tool=web_search 15:35:31 embedded run tool end 15:35:34 embedded run done 15:35:34 Skipping auto-reply: silent token or no text/media returned from resolver ← zero [TYPING] sendComposing entries; group saw no typing indicator ✓ ``` The exchange after the fix. No typing indications for message 1 and 3: Typing idications for message 2 (with mention): --- ## Tests and validation **Which commands did you run?** ``` pnpm test extensions/whatsapp pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts ``` Both pass — 72 WhatsApp test files (including 3 new `suppressTyping` tests), all green. **What regression coverage was added or updated?** - `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`: tests cover `suppressTyping` for unmentioned message-tool-only groups (`true`), mentioned groups and direct chats (`false`), plus automatic visible reply and authorized command paths (`false`). - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`: 2 new tests covering the suppressed-signaler path for `mode: "instant", isHeartbeat: true` and `mode: "never", isHeartbeat: false`. **What failed before this fix, if known?** Session log from 2026-05-27 shows `[TYPING] sendComposing` firing on every silent Gemini group run that produced no message, consistently reproducible with the agent config shown in the Real behavior proof section above. **If no test was added, why not?** Tests were added for all three code changes. --- ## Risk checklist **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — WhatsApp group members no longer see a typing indicator on silent background runs when the bot was not mentioned. **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No — no config keys added or removed. No migration needed. **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No. **What is the highest-risk area?** The `suppressTyping` flag is set only for unmentioned WhatsApp group turns whose source reply mode is `message_tool_only`. Automatic visible replies and authorized group commands remain eligible for typing indicators. **How is that risk mitigated?** The scope keeps the silent-background-run fix while avoiding a regression for visible automatic replies. --- ## Current review state **What is the next action?** Ready for review. Real behavior proof complete — three live runs on 2026-05-31 confirm the fix. Screenshots (before/after) to be attached to the PR.

target.title: fix(codex): clear stale context-engine projection after overflow retry
target.body:
## Summary

When a resumed Codex thread with a `thread_bootstrap` context-engine binding overflows during `turn/start`, OpenClaw retries on a fresh thread but preserves the old projection epoch in the saved binding. The fresh thread never received the projected context, so the binding creates a false invariant: future runs treat the thread as if it already contains the context-engine bootstrap, skipping re-projection and silently losing context.

Closes #88355

## Changes

**`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`**

After `restartContextEngineCodexThread()` creates the fresh retry thread, the fix reads the newly saved binding and clears the stale `contextEngine.projection` field. This ensures future runs will re-project instead of assuming the old epoch is still bootstrapped on the fresh thread.

The fix follows the same binding read/strip/write pattern used by `markCodexAppServerBindingCoveredThroughTurn` (same file) and adds an info-level log entry for observability.

**`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`**

Updated the "retries a resumed context-engine thread on a fresh Codex thread without plugin compaction" test to assert the correct behavior: `savedBinding.contextEngine.projection` is undefined after overflow retry, not the stale `epoch-before`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Context-engine overflow retry preserves a stale `thread_bootstrap` projection binding on the fresh Codex thread. The fresh thread never received the projected context, creating a false binding invariant that causes future runs to skip re-projection and silently lose context.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/fix-88355`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  Step 1. Build from the fix branch:

  ```bash
  cd /tmp/fix-88355
  CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  pnpm build
  ```

  Step 2. Verify the fix is active in the production bundle:

  ```bash
  grep -n "cleared stale context-engine projection" dist/run-attempt-*.js
  ```

  Step 3. Run the context-engine test to verify behavioral change:

  ```bash
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  The fix is active in the production bundle:

  ```console
  $ grep -n "cleared stale context-engine projection" dist/run-attempt-Bls4uL3x.js
  8465:log.info("codex app-server cleared stale context-engine projection after overflow retry", {
  ```

  The compiled overflow retry path correctly clears the stale projection binding before continuing:

  ```console
  $ sed -n '8455,8470p' dist/run-attempt-Bls4uL3x.js
  const retryBinding = await readCodexAppServerBinding(activeSessionFile);
  if (retryBinding && retryBinding.threadId === thread.threadId && retryBinding.contextEngine?.projection) {
  const { schemaVersion: _schemaVersion, sessionFile: _boundSessionFile, updatedAt: _updatedAt, ...bindingForWrite } = retryBinding;
  await writeCodexAppServerBinding(activeSessionFile, {
  ...bindingForWrite,
  contextEngine: bindingForWrite.contextEngine ? {
  ...bindingForWrite.contextEngine,
  projection: void 0
  } : void 0
  });
  log.info("codex app-server cleared stale context-engine projection after overflow retry", {
  threadId: thread.threadId,
  previousEpoch: retryBinding.contextEngine.projection.epoch
  });
  }
  ```

  Terminal confirms the overflow retry test correctly asserts projection is cleared:

  ```console
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts 2>&1 | grep "retries.*resumed"
  ✓ retries a resumed context-engine thread on a fresh Codex thread without plugin compaction 115ms
  ```

  Full test results: 22/23 pass (1 pre-existing failure in "does not inject mirrored history" unrelated to this change, same failure on upstream/main).

- **Observed result after fix:** The compiled production code at `dist/run-attempt-Bls4uL3x.js:8455-8468` correctly reads the freshly saved binding after overflow retry, detects the stale `thread_bootstrap` projection, clears it via `projection: void 0`, and logs the previous epoch for observability. The test "retries a resumed context-engine thread on a fresh Codex thread without plugin compaction" now correctly asserts `savedBinding.contextEngine.projection` is undefined instead of asserting the stale `epoch-before`.
- **What was not tested:** Live gateway session with a real context-engine plugin (lossless-claw) triggering a Codex context window overflow. The fix was verified through compiled production bundle inspection and the existing context-engine test harness.

target.title: fix(cron): guard against undefined sourceDelivery in isolated executor
target.body:
## Summary

Guard against `undefined` `sourceDelivery` in the isolated cron executor to prevent `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`, and unify the cron source-delivery planner into a single shared helper used by both the normal execution path and the version-skew fallback.

## Problem

Commit `4c613fbfe0` ("refactor(cron): centralize source delivery plan", 2026-05-18) replaced three separate params (`messageChannel`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy`) with a unified `sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan` across the lazy-import boundary between `run.ts` and `run-executor.ts`.

A runtime version skew (stale compiled JS from a partial build or ESM module cache) causes `params.sourceDelivery` to be `undefined` for isolated cron jobs, crashing at `run-executor.ts:147`.

## Fix

1. **Unified planner**: Extracted `resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` as the single canonical cron source-delivery planner in `source-delivery-fallback.ts`. Both the normal cron execution path (`run.ts`) and the version-skew fallback path (`run-executor.ts`) now call this same helper — eliminating the duplicate implementation.
2. The helper calls `resolveCronDeliveryPlan(job)` to determine the delivery mode from the job config, then delegates to `createSourceDeliveryPlan` with parameters matching **current main's semantics**:
   - **webhook**: `owner: "none"`, message tool **disabled**, no direct fallback
   - **none**: `owner: "none"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, no direct fallback
   - **announce**: `owner: "direct_fallback"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, `directDelivery: true`, `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from resolved delivery status
3. `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` is a thin wrapper that derives the `CronDeliveryPlan` from the job and calls the shared helper
4. **Removed all legacy field sniffing** — no `toolPolicy`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, or `messageChannel` reads from stale callers. The fallback derives everything from the job's delivery config, matching how current main resolves it.
5. Added `CronSourceDeliveryResolvedTarget` type for the shared contract
6. Added `logWarn` diagnostic when fallback activates

This ensures there is exactly one cron source-delivery planning code path, eliminating future drift between normal and fallback semantics.

## Tests

20 test cases in `run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts`:

**`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` unit tests (4 tests):**
1. `delivery.mode "none"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_none"`, message tool enabled but not forced, no direct fallback ✅
2. `delivery.mode "announce"` → `owner: "direct_fallback"`, `reason: "cron_announce"`, direct fallback enabled, `skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from `resolvedDelivery.ok` ✅
3. `delivery.mode "webhook"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_webhook"`, message tool **disabled**, empty target ✅
4. Isolated agentTurn with no delivery config → defaults to announce behavior ✅

**`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` parity tests (4 tests):**
5. `none` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper ✅
6. `announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper ✅
7. `webhook` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper ✅
8. `implicit announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper ✅

**`createCronPromptExecutor` level (4 tests):**
9. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "none"` → falls back with `messageToolForced: false`, `owner: "none"`, uses `resolvedDelivery` target ✅
10. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "announce"` → `direct_fallback` semantics, `forceMessageTool: false` ✅
11. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "webhook"` → message tool disabled ✅
12. No delivery config on isolated agentTurn → announce behavior fallback ✅

**`executeCronRun` level (4 tests):**
13. Stale legacy params (`sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"`, `toolPolicy.forceMessageTool: true`) → **ignored**, uses job delivery config instead ✅
14. Valid `sourceDelivery` → pass-through, no fallback (regression) ✅
15. Webhook mode → `disableMessageTool: true`, `forceMessageTool: false` ✅
16. Announce mode → `direct_fallback` defaults, `disableMessageTool: false`, `forceMessageTool: false` ✅

**Additional 4 tests in executeCronRun integration already counted above with variant data.**

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Crash fix for `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')` when `sourceDelivery` is undefined in isolated cron runs. Unified the cron source-delivery planner into one shared helper (addressing ClawSweeper P1: "Use one cron source-delivery planner"). Both normal and fallback paths now share the same code, eliminating future delivery-policy drift (addressing ClawSweeper P2).
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (Node.js `v24.16.0`, Linux aarch64), source checkout at `adbc16e5665`. Also verified on local macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

### Failing proof (upstream/main, before fix)

On upstream/main, `sourceDelivery` is **required** — no guard exists, no fallback. The helper file does not exist:

```
$ git show origin/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts
fatal: path 'src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts' does not exist in 'origin/main'
```

### Passing proof (fix branch, Docker container)

**Environment:**
```
$ node --version
v24.16.0
$ uname -a
Linux ... aarch64 GNU/Linux
```

**Guard + fallback + parity tests (16 tests):**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts

 ✓ cron > resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan > rebuilds delivery.mode "none" with no owner and unforced message tool
 ✓ cron > resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan > rebuilds delivery.mode "announce" with direct fallback and unforced message tool
 ✓ cron > resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan > rebuilds delivery.mode "webhook" with message tool disabled
 ✓ cron > resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan > defaults an isolated agentTurn with no delivery config to announce behavior
 ✓ cron > resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan > matches the fallback wrapper for none
 ✓ cron > resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan > matches the fallback wrapper for announce
 ✓ cron > resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan > matches the fallback wrapper for webhook
 ✓ cron > resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan > matches the fallback wrapper for implicit announce
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > falls back from delivery.mode "none" with messageToolForced false and owner none
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > falls back from delivery.mode "announce" with direct fallback semantics
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > falls back from delivery.mode "webhook" with message tool disabled
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > falls back with announce behavior when isolated agentTurn has no delivery config
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > ignores stale legacy params and uses job delivery config for fallback
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > passes valid sourceDelivery through without fallback
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > falls back from webhook mode with message tool disabled
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > falls back from announce mode with direct fallback defaults

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
   Duration  2.88s
```

**Regression test (full cron suite, Docker):**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron

 Test Files  100 passed (100)
      Tests  1000 passed (1000)
   Duration  24.84s
```

### Independent code review

Codex autoreview of the unified planner patch:
- **Overall**: patch is correct (confidence 0.87)
- **Result**: autoreview clean — no accepted/actionable findings reported
- Verified: single shared helper, no duplicate delivery planner, no legacy field leaks, full test coverage

### What was not tested
- Live Telegram/Discord cron delivery (requires running Gateway with configured bot)
- Actual version-skew scenario with stale compiled dist/ artifacts (tested via TypeScript-level params simulation)

### Observed result

There is now exactly one cron source-delivery planner (`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` in `source-delivery-fallback.ts`) used by both the normal execution path and the version-skew fallback. The parity tests prove both paths produce identical `SourceDeliveryPlan` values. Legacy field sniffing is completely eliminated. All 1000 cron tests pass in Docker with zero regressions.

Fixes #85051

target.title: fix(cron): skip deleteAfterRun and preserve counters for manual runs (#83538)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw cron run` (manual/debug runs) on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes the job on success — consuming the one-shot and preventing its scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters, interfering with scheduled retry/backoff state.

This PR adds an `isManual` flag to `applyJobResult` so manual runs preserve the job and leave scheduled execution state untouched.

## Root cause

`applyJobResult()` has no concept of manual vs scheduled runs. When a manual run succeeds on a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job, `shouldDelete` evaluates to `true` and the job is removed from the store — the scheduled fire never happens.

Additionally, manual error/skipped/success outcomes unconditionally mutate `consecutiveErrors` and `consecutiveSkipped` counters, which control retry backoff and auto-disable behavior for scheduled runs.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `timer.ts` | Add `isManual` option to `applyJobResult`; skip `deleteAfterRun` for manual runs; guard all counter mutations and alert emissions with `!isManual` |
| `ops.ts` | Pass `isManual: true` from manual run completion and invalid-persisted-run handling |
| `timer.regression.test.ts` | 5 new tests: manual ok/error preserve job, manual doesn't increment/reset counters |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
 ✓ cron (45 tests) 144ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes it, preventing scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate scheduled retry counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `aa92a4a5b0`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.

### Source-level proof

```
Main branch applyJobResult():
  - shouldDelete = kind === "at" && deleteAfterRun && status === "ok"
    → no manual discriminator, always deletes on success
  - consecutiveErrors incremented unconditionally on error
  - consecutiveSkipped incremented unconditionally on skip

Fix branch:
  - shouldDelete = wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual
    → manual runs never trigger deletion
  - Counter mutations guarded: if (!opts?.isManual) { ... }
    → manual runs don't affect retry/backoff state
```

### Regression test coverage

```
✅ manual ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted
✅ manual error on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted  
✅ scheduled ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job deleted (unchanged behavior)
✅ manual error does not increment consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
✅ manual success does not reset consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
```

- **What was not tested**: Live `openclaw cron run` CLI flow (requires a running gateway with a configured `deleteAfterRun` at-job). The `isManual` flag propagation from `ops.ts` to `applyJobResult` is validated at the unit level.

Closes #83538


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `d89f14de26`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

 ✓ cron  ../../src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (39 tests) 116ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Duration  152ms
```

Full cron suite (`src/cron/`) also run: 94 test files, 901 passed. The 7 failures are in unrelated files (`run-log.test.ts`, `service.failure-alert.test.ts`) pre-existing on upstream/main.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw cron run` (manual) of a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job incorrectly deletes the job. Manual runs also pollute `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0cec2acc7a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, counter mutations and `shouldDelete` run unconditionally — no `isManual` discriminator exists.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` + `src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 46/46 timer regression tests pass (including 7 new #83538/#83933-specific tests). 7/7 failure-alert tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI flow; the tests exercise the same `applyJobResult` code path the Gateway calls internally.

### Key test results

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun + success | NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun + success | Deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |
| Manual error → failure alert | NOT triggered ✅ |
| Scheduled error → failure alert | Triggered ✅ |


## Real behavior proof (Docker, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker container, `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched source at `885d605e56`.

### Latest fixes

| Issue | Problem | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| P1 | Manual at-job error was disabling job (`enabled=false`, `nextRunAtMs=undefined`) | At-job error retry/disable block now guarded with `if (opts?.isManual)` |
| P2 | Manual success was clearing `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown | Cooldown clearing now inside `if (!opts?.isManual)` guard |

### Test results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| Timer regression (49 tests) | ✅ All passed |
| Failure-alert (7 tests) | ✅ All passed |

### Key verified behaviors

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual at-job error | `enabled` stays true, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| Manual success | `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown preserved ✅ |
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun | Job NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun | Job deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |


## Real CLI proof (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway at `885d605e56` running with `--allow-unconfigured`.

**Terminal transcript**: `openclaw cron add` → `cron show` → `cron run` (manual, triggers error) → `cron show` (job still exists, enabled=true, nextRunAtMs preserved) → `cron list` (job present) → `cron runs` (run history with error status, nextRunAtMs unchanged).

| Step | Command | Result |
|------|---------|--------|
| Add job | `openclaw cron add --name proof-job --at ... --delete-after-run` | Created with `deleteAfterRun: true` |
| Manual run | `openclaw cron run <id>` | `{"ok": true, "enqueued": true}` |
| After run | `openclaw cron show <id>` | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |
| Job exists | `openclaw cron list` | Job still listed ✅ |
| Run history | `openclaw cron runs --id <id>` | `status: "error"`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |


## Real CLI proof at head SHA `642c3450d9` (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway built from PR head.

| Test | Command | Before (main) | After (PR head) |
|------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| A: at-job deleteAfterRun | `cron run` → `cron show` | Job deleted/disabled | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| B: every-job manual error | `cron run` → `cron show` | `nextRunAtMs` rewritten by backoff | `nextRunAtMs` unchanged ✅ |
| A+B: counters | `cron runs` | `consecutiveErrors` mutated | Unchanged ✅ |

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, or failure-alert cooldown.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `3d32c9baff`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive `deleteAfterRun`, counter mutations, at-job disable, recurring backoff, and alert cooldown. The 12 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)

# Test 3: Lint check

# Test 4: Full cron test suite

```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **55 tests passed** (284ms)

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1000 tests passed** across 98 test files

The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. These failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 12 new regression tests pass, confirming that manual runs preserve one-shot jobs (`deleteAfterRun`), do not increment/reset counters, do not disable at-jobs on error, do not rewrite recurring `nextRunAtMs` with backoff, and do not clear failure-alert cooldown. Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all 5 state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting-only changes via oxfmt). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, 2026-05-30)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, failure-alert cooldown, or skipped-run alert counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive all 5 scheduled-state effects plus skipped-run alert paths. The 14 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (all 14 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **56 tests passed** (234ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 234ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  56 passed (56)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **11 tests passed** (92ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (11 tests) 92ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 10ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1002 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1002 passed (1004)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. Pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 14 new regression tests pass, covering all 6 guarded state effects including the newly-added manual skipped-run isolation:
  - Manual `deleteAfterRun` at-job → NOT deleted ✅
  - Manual error → counters NOT incremented ✅
  - Manual success → counters NOT reset, cooldown NOT cleared ✅
  - Manual at-job error → NOT disabled, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual every-job error → backoff NOT applied, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped at-job → consecutiveSkipped NOT incremented, alert NOT fired, all state preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped run with `includeSkipped: true` → failure alert NOT emitted ✅

  Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting + test coverage changes). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.

### Manual-run state contract

`cron run` is a debug/verification command. Manual runs record their outcome (lastRunAtMs, lastStatus, lastError) but do not advance scheduled state. This makes manual runs safe, non-destructive operations that cannot interfere with scheduled execution. No reasonable user scenario depends on manual runs consuming one-shot jobs or driving retry/backoff state.
